examples.title: Pumping Lemma Proof Structure for Non-Regularity
examples.description: General algorithm/template for proving language non-regular using Pumping Lemma
examples.code:
PROVE_NON_REGULAR(Language L, String s):
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Assume L is regular
 ASSUME L is regular
 
 // Step 2: Get pumping length p
 p ← pumping_length(L) // Exists by Pumping Lemma
 
 // Step 3: Choose string w in L with |w| ≥ p
 w ← choose_string(L, p) // Must satisfy conditions
 
 // Step 4: Consider all decompositions
 FOR each decomposition w = xyz WHERE |xy| ≤ p AND |y| > 0 DO
 found_counterexample ← FALSE
 
 // Try pumping up or down
 FOR i ← 0 TO 3 DO
 IF xy^iz ∉ L THEN
 found_counterexample ← TRUE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF found_counterexample THEN
 RETURN "L is not regular (Pumping Lemma violated)"
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN "Cannot prove non-regularity with this string"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the structure of a Pumping Lemma proof. The key is finding a string w and showing that no matter how we decompose it (with constraints), at least one pumped string falls outside L.

examples.title: Check Distinguishability for Myhill-Nerode
examples.description: Algorithm to check if two strings are distinguishable with respect to a language
examples.code:
def distinguishable(x, y, language_L):
 '''
 Check if strings x and y are distinguishable w.r.t. language L
 Returns True if ∃z such that exactly one of xz, yz ∈ L
 '''
 # Check all possible suffixes z (finite check for practical purposes)
 for z in generate_all_suffixes(max_length=5):
 xz_in_L = (x + z) in language_L
 yz_in_L = (y + z) in language_L
 
 if xz_in_L != yz_in_L: # Exactly one in L
 return True, z # Distinguishable, z is witness
 
 return False, None # Indistinguishable

def find_distinguishable_set(strings, language_L):
 '''
 Find if given set has pairwise distinguishable strings
 '''
 distinguishable_pairs = []
 
 for i in range(len(strings)):
 for j in range(i + 1, len(strings)):
 is_dist, z = distinguishable(strings[i], strings[j], language_L)
 if is_dist:
 distinguishable_pairs.append((strings[i], strings[j], z))
 
 return distinguishable_pairs
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the concept of distinguishability in Myhill-Nerode. Two strings are distinguishable if there exists a suffix that differentiates their membership in L.
